mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the unemployment rate below 5% signals tight labor market conditions that boost corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE < 5%, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns drops below 0.25.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production above 1% indicates strengthening manufacturing activity, which drives equity earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of cases where INDPRO rises > 1%, or the predictive power of INDPRO drops below 0.15 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and real sales of durable goods signals robust consumer demand, which drives equity valuations higher through improved earnings expectations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of HOUST and RSAFS on SPX returns is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10), or SPX returns are negative when both indicators exceed thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: A monetary tightening cycle characterized by rising federal funds rates above 4% compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS > 4%, or the correlation between FEDFUNDS and SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in payroll growth above 100k per month signals inflationary pressures that prompt central bank rate hikes, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in 50%+ of cases where PAYEMS rises > 100k, or the predictive power of PAYEMS on 5d returns drops below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in GDP below 1% signals economic weakness that reduces corporate earnings prospects, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in 50%+ of instances where GDPC1 declines > 1%, or the correlation between GDPC1 and SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production below 1% indicates weakening manufacturing activity, which reduces equity earnings growth and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in 50%+ of cases where INDPRO falls > 1%, or the predictive power of INDPRO on SPX returns drops below 0.15.
